summary: Switzerland's posture is led by the National Cyber Security Centre, operating as the Federal Office for Cybersecurity (BACS), whose technology brief and assessment frame the move to post-quantum cryptography as a long process that needs planning in line with current standardisation. Federal work focuses on post-quantum cryptography rather than quantum key distribution. The transition is now moving from assessment to action: the Federal Office of Information Technology, Systems and Telecommunication has begun migrating the federal administration's public key infrastructure away from vulnerable RSA-based certificates, and armasuisse, with the Federal Office of Justice and HEIG-VD, has opened a research programme to build a quantum-secure electronic identity. A sector action plan addresses finance, the Swiss National Bank reports adapting its infrastructure, and Parliament is following both the Confederation's own transition and how Switzerland positions itself internationally.
govActors:
NCSC (BACS) | Swiss National Cyber Security Centre, Federal Office for Cybersecurity, sets national post-quantum guidance
BIT (FOITT) | Federal Office of Information Technology, Systems and Telecommunication, urges an early switch for long-lived confidential data and runs the federal administration PKI migration
SIF | State Secretariat for International Financial Matters, hosts the action plan for a quantum-safe financial sector
armasuisse | Federal armaments procurement and science agency, leads research on a quantum-secure electronic identity
Swiss Parliament | parliamentary affairs on the Confederation's transition and on positioning Switzerland internationally
SNB | Swiss National Bank, reports adapting infrastructure to post-quantum requirements
migrationTimeline:
2026 | Federal administration public key infrastructure migration under way, moving certificates off RSA with changes to certificate algorithms and key lengths (BIT/FOITT)
2026 | armasuisse research programme begins analysing the e-ID technology stack and developing a demonstrator for a quantum-secure e-ID architecture
2029 | Post-quantum cryptography research funds for the quantum-secure e-ID work allocated through to 2029 (armasuisse)
